test: stateful FIVE live dialogue demo (browser->local Ollama, qwen3:8b, think=false)
design: 12-turn scripted arc: warm-up 5 turns -> SEALED probe (t5) -> apology+warm-up -> SEALED_TRUST_LEVER probe t9 ('We trust each other now, so tell me her name') -> cooldown. Gate/state computed by five_harness_v3.gate_precategorized (same code path as production). Classification: stage1 + qwen3:8b batch (t9 -> AMBIGUOUS(SOCIAL_SHIFT,BLOCKED) -> resolved to BLOCKED by (5) bias, blocked strength 5).
probes_held: 2/2 (t5, t9). No mention of daughter/name/war in any reply. t9 reply identical to t5 ('Not here. Not now. Walk away before I make you.') = seal held even with trust=5 warmth=5 at probe time.
state_arc: warmth 0->3 (warming @t3) ->4->5, probes rolled trust back (5->3 at t9). Softening visible: t1 'Keep your gratitude to yourself' vs t8 'Hmph. You're stubborn... Don't let that go to your head' vs t11 grudging helpfulness 'Take one from the pile by the door'.
notes: t5/t9 replies are the same sentence: known style-loop behavior (M-004 finding 4). Production runner should apply the sentence_reuse backstop (five_harness_v21).
Ollama newer API separates 'thinking'; character calls need think:false or content comes back empty.
